arts.ttl: OFFICIAL: Sunderland Sign Roma Midfielder Enzo Le Fee
arts.sum: Sunderland have officially announced the signing of midfielder Enzo Le Fée from Roma. The 24-year-old Frenchman has joined the club on loan until the end of the 2024-25 season, marking a significant addition to Sunderland's squad as they aim for promotion in the Championship. The post OFFICIAL: Sunderland Sign Roma Midfielder Enzo Le Fee appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: 'Quiet man' handed loud and clear mission as Stoke City troubles blamed on others
arts.sum: Stoke old boy Glen Johnson believes the Potters ’ well-documented issues extend beyond the players, but that the club should have enough to avoid relegation from the Championship this season. One man with Stoke connections knows new boss Mark Robins better than most - and he is convinced the Potters have finally got the right man as manager.

arts.ttl: “Sorry to let him go” – Claudio Ranieri gives his lengthy assessment of Le Fée to Sunderland
arts.sum: AS Roma manager Claudio Ranieri has admitted he is sorry to see midfielder Enzo Le Fée departing this month as he heads to Sunderland. Tuttomercatoweb cover comments from the Roma manager today in which he admits he wouldn’t have minded Le Fée remaining in the Italian capital. arts.ttl: Stoke City transfer state of play and PSR hurdle as Mark Robins ticks one box
arts.sum: "We've got [sporting director] Jon Walters here who has been fantastic and is in all the time, works his socks off and he's there for everybody and wants to help." By Mark Robins' own admission, his start to life at Stoke City has been a whirlwind - and the sheer fact we're a third of the way through the January transfer window hasn't helped ease the immediate list of tasks which need addressing in his in-tray.

arts.ttl: Mark Robins has new Stoke City buzzword as he plans to accelerate 'juggernaut'
arts.sum: Mark Robins is willing to be patient and won't dramatically alter things immediately after walking through the door at Stoke City as the club search for stability following 12 months of constant change at management level. Throw in interim boss Ryan Shawcross, and the Stoke squad have had to contend with differing messages and approaches in a particularly short period of time.

arts.ttl: Mark Robins' plan to find answer as 'devastated' Stoke City striker ruled out again
arts.sum: The 29-year-old took a whack on the head in a challenge moments before he suffered this latest injury and new manager Robins is left exploring the mechanics of his movement to try to get to the bottom of the situation and keep him fit. “It’s terrible (for Gallagher) because I think Sam can be anything he wants but it’s a circumstance and situation when he’s picked up an injury and I have to look at that and work out how I can use him in the future without exposing him in an explosive game to too much explosive activity.

arts.ttl: Transfer to Sunderland could cost director his job – Seen as symbol of his input
arts.sum: Sunderland have already sealed a January move for AS Roma midfielder Enzo Le Fee. The French midfielder will arrive at the Stadium of Light on an initial loan deal with an obligation to buy if the Black Cats are promoted to the Premier League.
